title: Voice Question
description: Defines a single question in the voice elicitation interview engine. Each question belongs to a module, has a specific type, optional response options, follow-up branching logic, scoring rules, and rich metadata for sequencing and bias mitigation.

question_id.description: Unique identifier for this question. Standard modules use Mxx-Qnn format; semantic differential uses SD-nn format.

module_id.description: Identifier of the module this question belongs to. Standard modules use Mxx format; SD denotes the screening/demographic module.

module_name.description: Human-readable name of the module (e.g., 'Formality & Register', 'Emotional Tone').

text.description: The question text presented to the participant during the interview.

type.description: The response format type. Determines how the question is rendered and how responses are captured.

options.description: Available response options for closed-ended question types (likert, forced_choice, select, select_multiple, semantic_differential).

value.description: Machine-readable value stored when this option is selected.

label.description: Human-readable label displayed to the participant.

follow_up.description: Conditional follow-up question triggered by a specific response to this question.

condition.description: The condition that must be met for the follow-up to trigger. Can be a specific value, a comparison expression, or a boolean rule.

question_id.description: The question_id of the follow-up question to present when the condition is met.

scoring.description: Scoring configuration that maps responses to voice profile dimensions.

dimensions.description: List of voice profile dimension keys this question contributes to.

weights.description: Dimension-keyed weights indicating relative contribution of this question to each dimension score.

scoring_map.description: Maps response values to numeric scores per dimension. Keys are response values (as strings); values are objects mapping dimension names to scores.

metadata.description: Rich metadata governing question presentation, sequencing, bias mitigation, and timing.

self_reportability_tier.description: How reliably participants can self-report on this dimension. Tier 1 = high accuracy, Tier 4 = low accuracy (requires behavioral/observational measurement).

finding_refs.description: References to research findings that inform this question's design.

bias_mitigation.description: Description of the bias mitigation strategy applied to this question (e.g., 'reversed anchoring', 'indirect phrasing', 'scenario-based').

branching.description: Branching constraints that control when this question is included or excluded based on the participant's writer type path.

required_branches.description: Writer type branches that must be active for this question to appear.

excluded_branches.description: Writer type branches for which this question is skipped.

position.description: Positioning hints for question ordering within the interview flow.

module_sequence.description: Ordinal position of this question within its module.

estimated_seconds.description: Estimated time in seconds for a participant to read and respond to this question.

format_category.description: Category label for format rotation tracking. Used to prevent monotony by avoiding consecutive questions of the same format.
